Compound assignment operators combine arithmetic operations with assignment in numeric expressions.
Algorithms are step-by-step processes represented in language or diagrams.
Data types are value sets with operations, categorized as primitive or reference.
Selection statements alter normal sequential flow of code execution.
System.out.println moves cursor to new line; System.out.print does not.
Variables require initialization with compatible types before use in expressions.
Use (int) and (double) operators to convert between primitive types.
APIs provide documented specifications for using library classes organized in packages.
Comments explain code to programmers; three Java types serve documentation purposes.
Methods are named code blocks; procedural abstraction enables usage without understanding implementation.
Class methods use static keyword and belong to the class, not instances.
Math class from java.lang package is automatically available without import.
Classes are blueprints; objects are specific instances with defined attributes.
Constructors are class methods with the same name that create objects.
Use dot operator with object name to invoke instance methods on class objects.
String objects represent character sequences, created via literals or constructors.
Algorithms are built from sequencing, selection, and repetition.
Equality operators compare primitive values or object references.
Nested if statements place conditional structures within other conditional structures.
Equivalent Boolean expressions produce identical results; truth tables prove equivalence.
Iteration repeats code while a Boolean condition remains true.
for loops contain initialization, Boolean expression, and update components.
Public and private keywords control access to class members and encapsulate implementation details.
Standard algorithms solve divisibility, digit extraction, frequency, extrema, sum, and average problems.
Standard algorithms search, count, and reverse substrings with specific properties.
Inner loops complete all iterations before outer loop advances.
Abstraction reduces complexity by focusing on main ideas and hiding irrelevant details.
System reliability requires testing programs under varied conditions to ensure consistent performance.
Object state comprises attributes defined by instance variables.
Void methods have no return value; void keyword precedes method name.
Object references passed as parameters share the same object; modifications affect original.
Local variables declared in blocks are only accessible within that block.
this references the current object in instance methods and constructors.
Arrays store multiple same-type values: primitives or object references.
Files store persistent data retrievable during program execution.
Integer and Double wrapper classes are immutable objects from java.lang.
ArrayList objects change size dynamically and store object references.
Use iteration or recursion to access ArrayList elements in sequence.
Standard ArrayList algorithms use traversals for finding extrema, aggregation, filtering, counting, and element manipulation.
2D arrays are arrays of arrays with fixed size, storing primitive or reference data.
Nested loops traverse 2D arrays in row-major, column-major, or custom orders.
Standard algorithms use 2D traversals for searching, aggregating, counting, and manipulating elements.
Linear search checks elements sequentially until target found or exhausted.
Selection and insertion sort are iterative algorithms for sorting collections.
Recursive methods call themselves with base cases that halt execution.
Recursion traverses strings, arrays, and ArrayLists effectively.
Autoboxing automatically converts primitives to wrapper objects in assignments and method calls.
Constructors allocate memory, return references, initialize all instance variables.
Removing elements during traversal needs special techniques to prevent skipping.
Enhanced for loops traverse rows as 1D arrays; reassigning loop variables doesn't modify arrays.
Methods return object references directly, not copies of objects.
Array length is fixed at creation and accessible via length attribute.
Attributes store class data in variables; behaviors define class actions via methods.
Selection sort repeatedly finds extremum and swaps into final position.
Post-increment and post-decrement operators modify numeric variables by adding or subtracting one.
Calling methods on null references throws NullPointerException.
String class from java.lang package is available without explicit import.
Call class methods using ClassName.method(); class name optional within defining class.
Double to int casting removes decimal point digits without rounding.
Assignment operator stores right-side expression value into left-side variable.
Selection uses decision making; repetition uses looping in algorithms.
Preconditions must hold before method execution; method assumes they are met.
Literal represents fixed value in code.
Relational operators determine ordering relationships between numeric values.
If statements execute code segments conditionally based on Boolean values.
Math class provides static methods: abs, pow, sqrt, and random for mathematical operations.
Inner condition evaluation depends on outer condition being true first.
De Morgan's law transforms negated AND/OR expressions into equivalent forms.
Parameters enable value passing; signatures include method name and ordered parameter types.
Infinite loops result when the Boolean expression never becomes false.
Superclasses contain common attributes; subclasses inherit without replacing code.
Each recursive call maintains separate local variables tracking progress.
Complex algorithms may traverse multiple String, array, or ArrayList objects concurrently.
Initialization executes once; loop control variable evaluated, body executes, update runs, repeat.
New 2D arrays initialize elements to default values by type.
Data abstraction separates abstract properties from implementation details, managing complexity.
Sequencing establishes the order in which process steps execute.
File and Scanner classes connect programs to file data.
Primitive types define values and operations for numbers and Booleans.
Programs impact society and economy with both intended benefits and unintended harmful consequences.
Classes in this course are declared public using the class keyword.
ArrayList() constructor creates an empty list with no elements.
Constructor signatures include the class name and ordered parameter types.
Linear search on 2D arrays requires accessing each row individually first.
Non-void methods return single values; header specifies return type.
this can pass the current object as a method argument.
Local variables shadow instance variables when names conflict.
Binary search requires sorted data, halves search space per recursive call.
Copy mutable constructor parameters to prevent unintended state modifications.
Reference types define non-primitive objects.
for loops and while loops are functionally equivalent and interchangeable.
Class methods lack access to the this reference.
Recursive and iterative solutions are interchangeable and equivalent.
Return expressions must match declared return type; called return by value.
Multiple constructors with different signatures are called overloaded constructors.
One-way if executes code only when the condition evaluates true.
Binary search outperforms linear search in efficiency.
Constructors in this course are always designated public.
Create File objects with pathname string to open files.
2D array initializers use nested lists representing 1D array rows.
If-else-if executes at most one code block based on first true condition.
Out-of-range index access throws IndexOutOfBoundsException.
Private members accessible only when parameter type matches enclosing class type.
Postconditions guarantee true results after method execution completes.
Every Java class inherits from the Object class at the top.
Use == and != operators to compare whether variables reference same object.
Math.random() output can be transformed to generate random values within specified inclusive/exclusive ranges.
Array elements default to zero, 0.0, false, or null by type.
Generic ArrayList<E> specifies element type; preferred for compile-time error detection.
Insertion sort places unsorted elements into correct sorted positions with shifts.
Void methods return no value and cannot be used in expressions.
Attributes are class data; instance variables are unique per object, class variables shared.
String objects are immutable; methods return new strings rather than modifying.
Loop body skips execution if initial Boolean condition is false.
Int values automatically widen to double in certain expressions.
Unboxing automatically converts wrapper objects to primitives in assignments and method calls.
Expressions evaluate to single values with types determined by evaluation.
IDEs provide integrated tools for writing, compiling, and running code.
Programmers must obtain permission and licensing for proprietary code; open source code is freely reusable.
Selection directs algorithm execution based on true or false conditions.
Relational operator expressions produce Boolean true or false results.
String literal is character sequence in double quotes.
Compilers detect errors that must be fixed before execution.
Non-void methods return typed values; return values must be stored or used in expressions.
Integer.parseInt() converts a String to an int value.
Reference variables store object memory addresses, not actual objects.
Reference variables hold object references or null values.
ArrayList requires import statement from java.util package for usage.
Concatenate strings with + or +=; primitives implicitly convert to strings.
Repetition executes a process repeatedly until desired outcome achieved.
If-else executes one branch when true, alternate branch when false.
Compare references to null using == or != to check object existence.
Do not add or remove elements in enhanced for loops to avoid ConcurrentModificationException.
Off-by-one errors result from iterating one time too many or few.
Brackets [row][col] access elements; first index is row, second is column.
Procedural abstraction names processes; method decomposition enables reuse and reduces duplication.
Each object has its own independent copy of instance variables.
Java provides default no-parameter constructor with type-specific default values.
Return transfers control back to caller; code after return never executes.
Initializer lists create and populate arrays with specified values.
Binary search implementations may be iterative or recursive.
Three primitive types: int (integers), double (real numbers), boolean (true/false).
Handle file opening errors with throws IOException declaration.
Escape sequences like \", \\, \n provide special meaning in strings.
Scanner class enables keyboard text input from multiple input sources.
Round doubles by adding/subtracting 0.5 before casting to int.
while loops evaluate conditions before each iteration, executing body when true.
Square brackets with index access and modify array elements.
Instance variables should be private to maintain encapsulation unless specified otherwise.
Integer.MAX_VALUE and Integer.MIN_VALUE define int range limits.
Single brackets access entire rows of a 2D array.
Parameters generalize procedures, enabling reuse with different input values.
Classes define equals methods to determine object equivalency based on attributes.
Syntax errors violate language rules and are compiler-detected.
Merge sort recursively sorts arrays and ArrayLists.
Objects are created using the new keyword and a constructor call.
Variables store mutable values with names and data types during execution.
Import java.io package to access File and IOException classes.
Order of sequencing, selection, and repetition affects algorithm outcome.
Double.parseDouble() converts a String to a double value.
Arithmetic expressions use numeric values, variables, operators as int or double.
ArrayList methods: size(), add(), get(), set(), remove() for list manipulation.
Concatenating objects calls their toString method; subclasses may override it.
Arguments must match parameter types in number and order; passed by value as copies.
Accessor methods return copies of instance or class variable values.
Default values: int→0, double→0.0, boolean→false, reference→null.
Ints occupy 4 bytes, restricting values to MIN_VALUE through MAX_VALUE.
ArrayList indices begin at zero and end at size minus one.
Method overloading allows same names with different parameter signatures.
Operators +, -, *, /, % determine result type based on operand types.
Scanner methods read file data: nextInt, nextDouble, nextBoolean, nextLine, next, hasNext, close.
Valid indices are 0 to length-1; invalid indices throw exception.
Mutator methods modify instance or class variable values; typically void.
Constructor parameters initialize object attributes with provided values.
Procedural abstraction allows internal method changes without affecting external users.
Public methods allow internal and external access; private methods allow only internal access.
String indices range from 0 to length-1; out-of-range access throws exception.
Use length attribute for dimensions; invalid indices cause ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
Logic errors cause incorrect behavior, detected through program testing.
Merge sort divides arrays into single elements, recursively merges sorted subarrays.
nextLine and other Scanner methods handle whitespace differently.
Integer overflow wraps values within range, producing unexpected results.
Key methods: length, substring, indexOf, equals, compareTo for string operations.
Method calls halt sequential flow, execute method statements, then return control afterward.
Design classes before implementation using natural language or diagrams.
Parameters receive values that methods use to accomplish their tasks.
Run-time errors occur during execution and cause abnormal termination.
Integer division truncates; double division returns full quotient.
Constructor arguments must match parameter types, order, and number via call by value.
Constructor calls interrupt execution, run constructor code, then return control flow.
Double precision limits cause round-off errors; use ints to avoid them.
Remainder operator % computes division remainder.
Exceptions are unexpected run-time errors interrupting program execution.
Primitive arguments passed by value; parameter changes don't affect argument.
Operator precedence and parentheses determine evaluation order in expressions.
Integer division by zero throws ArithmeticException.
